name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: Does a code review record (e.g. pull request review) exist for the change?
Is there evidence the code review was approved before the change was merged/committed to the main branch?
Is the code review approval timestamp before the merge/commit timestamp to the main branch?
Was the change prevented from merging to main until the review was completed?

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: Is the code reviewer(s) a different individual from the change author?
Does the approving reviewer have no authorship/committer role in the change under review?
Was the approval given by a party independent of the change author (4-eyes principle satisfied)?

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Do all unit tests pass with zero failures?
Do all integration tests pass?
Do critical user journey end-to-end tests pass?
Are performance benchmarks met (if applicable)?
Have security scan/test results been reviewed and approved?
Is unit test code coverage ≥ 80% for all new code?
Is integration test coverage ≥ 60% for critical integration points?
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN is coverage = 100% for those critical paths?
For legacy code, is existing coverage maintained or improved?
Is branch coverage ≥ 70%?
Is function coverage ≥ 80%?
Was a coverage report generated and reviewed as part of the code review process?
Did tests run in the CI/CD pipeline before merge?
Are test results visible in the pull request status checks?
Were flaky tests fixed or removed before release?
Does the pre-release checklist confirm no known critical or high-severity bugs?
Were manual smoke tests completed for critical features?

IF the change qualifies for a documented policy exception (legacy integration, third-party dependency, POC/experimental, documentation-only, build/deployment scripts, dependency version bumps, or refactoring with no behavioral change) THEN is the exception applicable and documented?
